commit.message:
opus-2026-03-05-S3: n=8 formula correction, general OCF formula, swap involution

- THM-013 simplified formula FAILS at n>=8 (VD 3-5 cycle pairs)
- Correct general formula: DH = sum 2^k * Delta(alpha_k), verified n=4,...,9
- OCF verified directly at n=8 (20 random tournaments)
- THM-014: swap involution gives adj(i,j)-adj'(j,i) = #U_T - #U_T'
  where blocking vertices have s_x = -1 (T) or +1 (T')
- Updated OPEN-Q-009, TANGENTS (T030, T031), SESSION-LOG

commit.message:
Add trophy system, raise level cap to 500, expand endgame loot

Trophy system (50 trophies, 9 categories):
- Boss: first kill, 10/50/100/500 kills, flawless victory
- Combat: 100/500/9999 damage, 100/1k/10k monster kills
- Death: first wipe, 10/100/500 wipes
- Level: 25/50/100/150/200/500, full house (5x lv50+)
- Loot: legendary finds, enchant +5/+10
- Progression: village, talisman, dungeons, recruits
- Crumbs: 10k/100k/1M/10M/100M earned
- Time: 1/10/100/999/9999 hours played
- Shop: buyable for 1M/5M/25M/100M crumbs

New [Trophies] tab in tavern with buy support.
Trophy checks run every 5s. Trophies sync via live-state.

Level cap raised from 50 to 500. Added 8 new loot prefixes
(Celestial/Primordial/Eternal/Void-Forged/Astral/Transcendent/
Godforged/Omega) for levels 35-400.

Tracks: boss kills, highest damage, legendaries found, enchant
level, total recruits, play time. Boss kills shown in dungeon
summary.

fix(straddle): move open call to very start of cycle with direct Binance fetch

The straddle open was buried 460 lines deep inside execute_trading_cycle(),
after history preload, parallel fetch, and regime detection — none of which
are needed for the straddle. Moved it to the very first thing the cycle does,
with its own direct Binance BTCUSDT ticker fetch, completely independent of
the rest of the trading pipeline. This bypasses whatever was preventing
execution from reaching the old location.

commit.message:
Add additive pricing mode with conditional discount rules

Vendors can now choose between two pricing modes:
- Combination: unique price per parameter combination (exponential)
- Additive: base price + per-option additions (linear)

Additive mode also supports conditional discount rules, e.g.
"When Material = Wood, total gets 10% off" or
"When Color = Red, Size = Large gets $5 off"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eliminate entry price drift — use close prices, not intraday

Three changes to prevent cumulative portfolio return distortion:

1. PaperBroker: removed random slippage from _get_fill_price. Fills
   now use the feed price directly (no ±0.1% random noise).

2. Live engine: added _reconcile_entry_prices() called at daily_open.
   Adjusts entry_price and avg_cost to the actual close of the entry
   date using historical data. Prevents pre-close fill prices
   (~15:30 ET) from drifting vs the close-based benchmark.

3. State files: corrected current positions to real close prices
   (MRK $119.83, JNJ $246.75, AMAT $351.32, LRCX $214.68,
   GOOGL $300.88). Net correction: -$224.70 in cost basis.

Fix purge FK violations, SQL injection, date validation, orphan cleanup, and bash classifier false positives

Six bugs found via fuzz-style testing:
- s3_purge: delete_session/delete_sessions_for_project failed with FK constraint
  violations because observations and prompts were not deleted before sessions
- s3_purge: build_obs_where interpolated --id directly into SQL instead of using
  parameterized queries
- s3_purge: parse_date_to_ts accepted invalid dates like April 31 and Feb 30
- s3_purge: cleanup_orphans didn't remove observations referencing deleted sessions
- s1_extract: contains_git_cmd double-split on && and ; caused false positives
  when "git" and a subcommand appeared in separate chained commands

Nine new tests covering all fixed bugs plus edge cases.

Add console.error logging to DebtModal save catch block

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CUDA OOM after auto-batch-size by running search in subprocess

Auto-batch-size binary search deliberately OOMs the GPU, corrupting
PyTorch's autograd graph and leaking ~132GB. Now runs in a separate
subprocess (_auto_batch_worker.py) so the CUDA driver frees all memory
on process exit. Also increases safety margin from 5% to 20% and adds
target tensor + loss to the synthetic test for accuracy.

commit.message:
fix: handle Range header in HEAD requests (#114)

## What I'm changing

The HEAD handler was ignoring Range headers entirely, always returning
200 OK with the full Content-Length. This caused clients issuing `HEAD
-H "Range: bytes=0-1023"` to receive incorrect responses missing 206
status, Content-Range, and Accept-Ranges headers.

## How I did it

- Extract range parsing into `parse_range_str`/`parse_range_header` in
utils/core.rs with comprehensive unit tests (13 test cases)
- Update HEAD handler to return 206 Partial Content with correct
Content-Range and Content-Length when a Range header is present
- Refactor GET handler to use the same shared parsing logic

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Staircase diphthong micro-frames to reduce shimmer

Smooth per-sample formant sweeping during diphthongs caused
harmonics to linger near formant peaks, creating audible
beating (shimmer). Fix: hold formants steady within each
micro-frame (endCf=NAN disables exponential smoothing) and
use short 3ms snap fades between steps. Formants jump quickly
to each waypoint then plateau, minimizing time spent near
harmonic-formant crossings. Fixed N=5 micro-frames balances
step size (no rubber-band compression) against transition
speed (no shimmer).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(hooks): resolve pre-commit path doubling when CWD is a subdirectory (#2976)

* fix(hooks): resolve pre-commit path doubling when CWD is a subdirectory

git returns paths relative to the repo root, but deno resolves them
relative to CWD. When a subagent cd's into e.g. packages/runner/, this
causes doubled paths like packages/runner/packages/runner/src/cell.ts.

Fix by:
1. Resolving repo root via git rev-parse --show-toplevel
2. Passing cwd: repoRoot to all Deno.Command calls
3. Resolving parsed command paths relative to the effective CWD
   (accounting for cd in the command) back to repo-root-relative

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(hooks): resolve pre-commit path doubling when CWD is a subdirectory

git returns paths relative to the repo root, but deno resolves them
relative to CWD. When a subagent cd's into e.g. packages/runner/, this
causes doubled paths like packages/runner/packages/runner/src/cell.ts.

Fix by:
1. Resolving repo root via git rev-parse --show-toplevel
2. Passing cwd: repoRoot to all Deno.Command calls
3. Resolving parsed command paths relative to the effective CWD
   (accounting for cd in the command) back to repo-root-relative
4. Restricting git-add and cd regex matching to before the git commit
   portion to avoid false matches inside commit messages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* simplify(hooks): remove cd/path resolution over-engineering from pre-commit

The essential fix is cwd: repoRoot on all Deno.Command calls. The cd
detection, effectiveCwd, and resolveToRepoRelative logic added complexity
for an edge case that the cwd fix already handles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(hooks): limit git-add regex to pre-commit segment of command

Prevents false-matching file paths inside commit message text.

commit.message:
Fix agent state bouncing: gateway detection + heartbeat tuning + worker cooldown (#10)

Three fixes for agent visualization stability:

1. Gateway detection via globalThis flag — when openclaw spawns task workers,
   it reloads plugins in the same process. The plugin's register() was
   re-running, resetting Lain to idle every time a task spawned. Now only
   the first load (gateway) registers agents and starts heartbeat/auto-spawn.

2. Heartbeat interval 30s → 120s — reduces unnecessary state reports while
   staying within the hub's 5-min cleanup window.

3. Worker cooldown (60s) after task completion — prevents rapid
   desk→idle→desk→idle bouncing when tasks fire in quick succession.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
